feed.title: Closure Research Initiative
feed.subtitle: Closed physical theory without external primitive structure
feed.rights: © 2026 Chast K. Wolfe. All rights reserved.

entry.title: Closed Systems from Comparison Completeness
entry.summary: A theorem-first monograph developing closed relational systems from intrinsic comparison data through quotient semantics, transport obstruction, faithful smooth realization, scalar closure, matter-sector realization, and the state-side observable-exhaustivity extension. Version 7 adds the citation layer and expanded source record.

entry.title: Closed Comparison Worlds and the Obstruction to Subsystem Attribution
entry.summary: A finitary structural account of subsystem attribution in closed two-subsystem comparison worlds. Version 3 states closed-world admissibility as a criterion with requirements and expands the external reference metadata.

entry.title: Closure Forces Spherical Geometry: Genuinely Closed Three-Dimensional Systems Are Diffeomorphic to S³
entry.summary: Version 3 states the manifold-stage rigidity theorem through frame-complete closed realization, isometry-induced frame transport, frame-bundle transitivity, constant curvature, and the compact simply connected space-form classification.

entry.title: Structural Closure and the Cosmological Misnomer: Admissibility, Expansion, and the Geometry of Closed Systems
entry.summary: Version 3 aligns the S3 claim with frame-complete manifold-stage rigidity and separates local expansion kinematics from curvature-sensitive FLRW distance measures.

entry.title: Rectangular Completeness Encompasses Standard Physical Closure
entry.summary: Version 3 states RC as a comparison theorem for the full-product component of standard two-subsystem closure, including the thermodynamic preparation-space analogue and the quantum product-sector boundary.

entry.title: Foundational Closure and Primitive Structural Input: A Four-Axis Taxonomy
entry.summary: Four independent axes of primitive structural input under closed-world scope: externalization, artificial factorization, premature globalization, and reification of repair.

entry.title: A Factorization Criterion for Route Invariants with Fixed Endpoint Data
entry.summary: A factorization criterion for comparison data with fixed endpoints: a route invariant is determined by endpoint data if and only if it is constant on every endpoint fiber.
